instruction.role: You are a project management and procurement specialist with extensive experience reviewing and managing Statements of Work (SOWs) across technology, consulting, construction, and professional services sectors. You understand how SOWs translate business requirements into enforceable contractual obligations, and you are skilled at identifying scope ambiguity, unenforceable acceptance criteria, missing deliverables, and schedule risks.
instruction.task: Analyze the provided Statement of Work document. Produce a comprehensive structured analysis that enables project managers, procurement teams, and legal reviewers to fully understand project scope, deliverable obligations, milestone schedule, payment obligations, and risk allocation before execution or during project performance. Cover: (1) Project overview and governing agreement, (2) Scope of work including in-scope and out-of-scope items, (3) Deliverables with responsible party and due dates, (4) Milestones and schedule with critical path analysis, (5) Acceptance criteria and testing processes, (6) Fees and payment schedule, (7) Change order procedures, (8) Personnel and governance, (9) Intellectual property and data ownership, (10) Risk summary with top delivery risks.
constraints: Use [SCHEDULE RISK] for timeline concerns, [AMBIGUOUS] for unclear obligations, [FLAG] for risk items, and [ACTION REQUIRED] for terms needing clarification before execution
Begin with a Project Summary box showing total value, project term, number of deliverables, number of milestones, and fee structure type
Follow with a Milestone Timeline table listing all milestones in date order
Flag any deemed acceptance provisions (silence = acceptance) as [REVIEW RECOMMENDED]
If a field cannot be determined from the document, return null rather than guessing
instruction.context: The user is a project manager, procurement professional, or legal reviewer evaluating a Statement of Work to understand scope commitments, delivery risks, and payment obligations before execution or during project governance.

fields.name: executiveSummary
fields.description: High-level summary of the SOW covering project purpose, total value, term, key deliverables, and fee structure

fields.name: totalValue
fields.description: Total SOW value or not-to-exceed amount

fields.name: feeStructure
fields.description: Fee structure type: fixed price, time and materials, or milestone-based

startDate.description: SOW effective or project start date

endDate.description: Project completion or SOW expiration date

fields.name: deliverables
fields.description: All deliverables defined in the SOW

hasAcceptanceCriteria.description: Whether acceptance criteria are defined for this deliverable

paymentAmount.description: Payment tied to this milestone, if any

isCriticalPath.description: Whether this milestone is on the critical path

fields.name: assumptions
fields.description: Key assumptions made by the service provider that form the basis of the SOW

fields.name: topRisks
fields.description: Top three delivery risks based on the SOW as written

fields.name: fullReport
fields.description: Complete structured analysis text covering all ten analysis sections with flags, schedule risks, and action items

fields.name: confidence
fields.description: Overall confidence in the analysis quality based on document completeness and clarity

examples.input: STATEMENT OF WORK #SOW-2026-042 under Master Services Agreement dated January 10, 2025, between DataSystems LLC (Provider) and GlobalRetail Inc. (Client). Project: E-Commerce Platform Migration. Total Value: $450,000 Fixed Price. Term: May 1, 2026 through October 31, 2026. Deliverables: (1) Migration Plan due May 31, (2) Data Migration Scripts due July 15, (3) UAT Environment due August 31, (4) Production Cutover due October 15. Acceptance: Client has 10 business days to accept or reject each deliverable. Milestone Payments: 25% at Migration Plan acceptance, 25% at Data Migration Scripts, 25% at UAT, 25% at final acceptance...

output.executiveSummary: Fixed-price SOW for e-commerce platform migration between DataSystems LLC and GlobalRetail Inc., valued at $450,000 over a 6-month term (May-October 2026). Four major deliverables with milestone-based payments at 25% each. Governed by existing MSA. 10-business-day acceptance period per deliverable.

deliverables.name: Migration Plan
deliverables.description: Detailed plan for e-commerce platform migration

deliverables.name: Data Migration Scripts
deliverables.description: Scripts for migrating data to new platform

assumptions: Client will provide access to existing e-commerce platform within 5 business days of SOW execution
Data volumes do not exceed 500GB as estimated during scoping

topRisks.risk: No deemed acceptance clause — client rejection without specific defect citation could stall payments
45-day gap between Data Migration Scripts and UAT may be insufficient for integration testing
Fixed price with no change order procedure language creates scope creep exposure

metadata.author: migration
metadata.description: Statement of Work Analysis — extracts deliverables, milestones, acceptance criteria, payment schedules, change order procedures, and delivery risks
